title: Fixing a Node.js Express Middleware Route with Incorrect Route Parameter Validation

statement.markdown: Write a Node.js Express middleware route to validate user input for a username. The route should handle GET requests to the `/users/:username` endpoint. A valid username should be at least 3 characters long and contain only alphanumeric characters. If the input is invalid, return a 400 Bad Request response with an error message. If the input is valid, return a 200 OK response with a JSON object containing the username.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the username to be validated
statement.outputFormat: Either a 200 OK response with a JSON object containing the username or a 400 Bad Request response with an error message
statement.notes: The route should handle GET requests only. The input string should be parsed and validated accordingly.

constraints.description: Minimum length of a valid username

examples.explanation: A valid username is passed to the route
A valid username is passed to the route
A valid username is passed to the route
An empty string is passed to the route
A username shorter than 3 characters is passed to the route
A username containing special characters is passed to the route

hints.text: Use the `regExp` pattern to match alphanumeric characters and underscores

editorial.title: Fixing a Node.js Express Middleware Route with Incorrect Route Parameter Validation
editorial.intuition: The route should use a regular expression to validate the username.
editorial.bruteForce: The route can be implemented by checking if the username is at least 3 characters long and contains only alphanumeric characters and underscores.
editorial.betterSolution: The route can be implemented by using a regular expression to match the username pattern.
editorial.optimalSolution: The route should use a regular expression to match the username pattern and return a 400 Bad Request response if the input is invalid.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The route should return a 200 OK response if the input is valid and a 400 Bad Request response if the input is invalid.
commonMistakes: Not using a regular expression to match the username pattern
Not checking if the username is at least 3 characters long

starterCode.javascript: const express = require('express'); const app = express(); app.get('/users/:username', (req, res) => { // TO DO: validate username });

buggyStarterCode.javascript: const express = require('express'); const app = express(); app.get('/users/:username', (req, res) => { const username = req.params.username; if (username.length >= 3) { res.json({ username }); } else { res.json({ username }); } });

solutions.javascript: const express = require('express'); const app = express(); app.get('/users/:username', (req, res) => { const username = req.params.username; if (!/^[a-zA-Z0-9]+$/.test(username) || username.length < 3) { res.status(400).json({ error: 'Invalid username' }); } else { res.json({ username }); } }); app.listen(3000, () => { console.log('Server listening on port 3000'); });
